rulings.Case Title: VERNA SMITH et al VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Board of Trustees of the California State University¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar as superseded by defendant¿s amended motion filed December 18, 2012 set for hearing on April 11, 2013. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Mr. Dyson's declaration is insufficient to allow defendant to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages. The Court finds alternatively that, had Defendant sustained its initial burden, Plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Plaintiffs' response to defendant's Separate Statement, facts nos. 11, 13-33, 35-37.) If a hearing is requested, it will be Friday February 22, 2013 at 9:30am in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT ALCOA PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Cantley's declaration creates a triable issue whether the sophisticated user defense applies. (Plaintiffs' response to defendant's Separate Statement, fact no. 6; plaintiffs' Separate Statement, facts nos. 6-13.) Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' premises owner/contractor liability claim fails. If a hearing is requested, it will be Friday February 22, 2013 at 9:30am in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT ROBERTSHAW CONTROLS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and alternate motion for summary adjudication regarding plaintiffs' negligence claims are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Dr. Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to decedent, specifically whether defendant knew or should have known that the products to which its employees exposed decedent contained asbestos. (Plaintiffs' response to defendant's Separate Statement, facts nos. 25, 27, 30-32; Plaintiffs' Separate Statement facts nos. 1-4.) Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. If a hearing is requested, it will be Friday February 22, 2013 at 9:30am in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT SB DECKING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the flooring removed in Mr. McClain's presence aboard the Kilauea was the same flooring previously installed by defendant. The court considered plaintiff's opposition and evidence. If a hearing is requested, it will be Friday February 22, 2013 at 9:30am in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT COSCO FIRE PROTECTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant failed to sustain its burden on the issue of whether Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Mr. Crossley's declaration fails to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. However, defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of substantial factor causation and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. McClain's risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. If a hearing is requested, it will be Friday February 22, 2013 at 9:30 am in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT AEROJET-GENERAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' premises owner/contractor liability cause of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Mr. Ross' deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs cannot prevail on their premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be Friday February 22, 2013 at 9:30 am in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT F.P. LATHROP CONSTRTUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Cantley's declaration creates a triable issue whether the sophisticated user defense applies. (Plaintiffs' response to defendant's Separate Statement, facts no. 38; plaintiffs' Separate Statement, facts nos. 6-13.) Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to decedent such that the negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action should be dismissed. Davis v. Foster Wheeler Energy Corp. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 731 is inapplicable. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that even if plaintiffs cannot demonstrate that defendant retained control over decedent's worksite, that plaintiffs' premises owner/contractor liability claim fails. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be Friday February 22, 2013 at 9:30am in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Intervenors on behalf of their suspended insured Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Intervenors sustained their initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Scott Co. rulings.Case Title: ROSE MARIE TAVARES, et al VS. HAMILTON MATERIALS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication of the negligence and products liability causes of action, defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony and declaration of Victor Tavares, Jr. and the declaration of Charles Ay create a triable issue whether decedent Victor Tavares was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Hamilton red dot joint compound) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Victor Tavares, Jr.¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are overruled as to paragraphs 16, 17, 18 and portions of 24 (13:22-28, 14:2-6) and sustained as to paragraphs 19-22 and portions of 24 (14:1-2). Even if Mr. Martin¿s declaration is considered, when read in conjunction with Victor Tavares, Jr.¿s declaration it merely creates a triable issue whether defendant sold, supplied, marketed or distributed Hamilton joint compound. As to summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery designed to elicit all facts and evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim for punitive damages, and by failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 14, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Dynalectric Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to submit evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Casey. Even accepting plaintiffs¿ argument that the duty was created pursuant to the 1955 General Industry Safety Orders (GISOs), plaintiffs failed to submit evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known that the dust allegedly disturbed by defendant¿s employees in Mr. Casey¿s presence was hazardous (i.e. asbestos) and exceeded the dust count acceptable under the 1955 GISOs. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declarations regarding whether the dust allegedly disturbed by defendant¿s employees exceeded the hazardous levels are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
